name: Kanban Board
description: Kanban board plugin for task management with UI, API endpoints, and agent automation
author: Evonic

cli_commands.name: kanban
cli_commands.description: Manage kanban tasks via CLI. Available subcommands: add, rm, update.

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Create a new kanban task. Title is required.

arguments.name: --assignee
arguments.help: Agent ID to assign the task to (optional)

subcommands.name: rm
subcommands.help: Delete a kanban task by ID
subcommands.description: Delete a kanban task by its numeric ID.

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.help: Update a kanban task by ID
subcommands.description: Update one or more fields of a kanban task.

arguments.name: --assignee
arguments.help: New assignee (agent ID, or 'none' to unassign)

variables.name: SUPER_AGENT_ID
variables.label: Super Agent ID
variables.description: Agent ID with full access to all kanban tasks and admin operations.

variables.name: ELIGIBLE_AGENTS
variables.label: Eligible Agent IDs (deprecated)
variables.description: DEPRECATED. Agent eligibility is now determined by the kanban skill — only agents with the kanban skill assigned (or the super agent) receive notifications. This field is ignored and kept only for backward compatibility.

variables.name: SCAN_INTERVAL_SECONDS
variables.label: Scan Interval (seconds)
variables.description: How often to scan the Kanban board for assigned tasks. Default: 300 (5 minutes).

variables.name: STALE_SCAN_INTERVAL_SECONDS
variables.label: Stale Task Scan Interval (seconds)
variables.description: How often to check for stale in-progress tasks that the agent lost track of. Default: 60 (1 minute).

variables.name: CHANNEL_TYPE
variables.label: Notification Channel Type
variables.description: Channel type to use for sending task notifications to agents (e.g. 'telegram').

variables.name: FINISH_REMINDER
variables.label: Finish Reminder
variables.default: Make sure to create a git commit summarizing the changes you made for this task.
variables.description: Reminder message injected into the tool response when an agent finishes a task (calls kanban:finish). Use this to enforce post-task actions like git commits, documentation updates, etc.

variables.name: CLEAR_CONTEXT_ON_NEW_TASK
variables.label: Clear Context on New Task
variables.description: Automatically clear the agent's chat history when activating a new task to prevent context bloat from previous tasks.

variables.name: TASK_DELAY_SECONDS
variables.label: Task Delay Before Notification (seconds)
variables.description: Minimum seconds a task must exist before being notified to an agent. This gives users time to correct or modify the task after creation. Default: 300 (5 minutes).

variables.name: ENABLE_PROCESS_RECORDER
variables.label: Enable Process Recorder
variables.description: Record the full agent execution process (thinking steps, tool calls, final response) when a task finishes. Viewable in the Process tab of task detail.
